title: Geometry and Dynamics of Domains in the Cotangent Bundles of Tori
speaker: Jun Zhang
affiliation: USTC-IGP (Institute of Geometry and Physics at the University of Science and Technology of China)
abstract: In Euclidean spaces, star-shaped domains (also known as Liouville domains) are fundamental objects in modern symplectic geometry. Several important subclasses have been introduced and studied, including dynamically convex domains, geometrically convex domains, and toric domains. These classes are not only interesting in their own right but also exhibit deep interrelations. In this talk, we extend this discussion to (fiberwise) star-shaped domains in the cotangent bundles of tori. In particular, we introduce analogous subclasses in this new setting and explore classical topics such as large-scale geometric properties and embedding problems. This talk is based on joint work with Antong Zhu.

proof_stages.label: Euclidean Liouville-domain benchmark
proof_stages.summary: The talk recalls Liouville domains, Reeb dynamics, global coarse symplectic distance and local capacities, then reviews the Euclidean subclasses—symplectically convex, dynamically convex, toric and monotone toric—and their known containment and separation phenomena.

proof_stages.label: Systolic convexity as the cotangent-bundle analogue of convexity
proof_stages.summary: For domains in T^*T^2 the speaker defines dynamic convexity, takes product domains as the toric analogue, rejects naive fiberwise convexity using low-index Finsler behavior, and introduces the class S of systolically convex domains by imposing dynamic convexity plus systolic ratio at most 1/4.

proof_stages.label: Reeb dynamics and systolic estimates for product domains
proof_stages.summary: Product domains are analyzed explicitly: their Reeb vector fields, closed orbits, actions and systolic ratios reduce to elementary geometry of the fiber, showing that product domains are dynamically convex and raising upper-bound questions for systolic ratios in product and dynamic-convex classes.

proof_stages.label: Containment diagram and strictness theorem
proof_stages.summary: After adding Finsler, flat and reversible subclasses to the cotangent-bundle picture, the first main theorem asserts that all non-definitional containment relations in the diagram are strict, witnessed by a collection of distinguishing constructions.

proof_stages.label: Large-scale richness of product domains
proof_stages.summary: The global theorem proves quasi-isometric embeddings of large Euclidean spaces into the space of product domains with the coarse symplectic distance, by transferring known Euclidean monotone-toric constructions through embeddings between shrunken toric domains and cotangent product domains.

proof_stages.label: Invariants distinguishing Euclidean toric and cotangent product worlds
proof_stages.summary: Although capacities agree under the toric-to-product bridge, Ruelle-type invariants and Hutchings' dynamical zeta function distinguish the corresponding domains and give unit-codisc examples that cannot be symplectomorphic to product domains.

proof_stages.label: Local capacity coincidence theorem and ECH proof strategy
proof_stages.summary: The local theorem states that for flat reversible Finsler domains satisfying either an extra symmetry or a small systolic-ratio bound, all ball-normalized capacities coincide and equal twice the systole; the proof reduces to a ball-embedding lower bound and verifies the needed ECH-capacity inequalities using Hutchings' lattice-polygon formula, Weitzenböck's inequality and Pick's theorem.
